petitioner_background: The petitioner is a transcultural management specialist seeking classification as an alien of extraordinary ability in business under section 203(b)(1)(A) of the Act. He has authored well-cited scholarly articles and books, held leading and critical roles for companies with widespread influence in South Africa and elsewhere in Africa, and contributed to the development of management education in South Africa and other African countries through contracts to organize high-level management seminars and the development of management and leadership resources and facilities in coordination with the South African Parliament.

evidence_per_criterion.evidence_submitted: The petitioner submitted evidence that his work has garnered media attention, though this was noted only 'to a lesser extent but worth noting.'
evidence_per_criterion.aao_analysis: The AAO acknowledged this evidence as worth noting but characterized it as being of lesser weight compared to the other evidence submitted. It was mentioned as one of the supporting elements in the overall finding of eligibility.

evidence_per_criterion.evidence_submitted: The petitioner submitted evidence of contributions of major significance to the development of management education in South Africa and other African countries, including contracts to organize high-level management seminars and for the development of management and leadership resources and facilities in coordination with the South African Parliament.
evidence_per_criterion.aao_analysis: The AAO found this evidence demonstrated the petitioner's contributions of major significance in the field of transcultural management, contributing to the conclusion that he has risen to the very top of his field.

evidence_per_criterion.evidence_submitted: The petitioner submitted extensive evidence documenting his well-cited scholarly articles and books in the field of transcultural management.
evidence_per_criterion.aao_analysis: The AAO found the petitioner's scholarly articles and books to be well-cited, supporting the finding that he has sustained national or international acclaim in his field.

evidence_per_criterion.evidence_submitted: The petitioner submitted evidence of his leading and critical role for companies with widespread influence in South Africa and elsewhere in Africa.
evidence_per_criterion.aao_analysis: The AAO found this evidence demonstrated that the petitioner performed in a leading or critical role for organizations with distinguished reputation and widespread influence, supporting eligibility under this criterion.

outcome_reasoning: The AAO sustained the appeal, finding that the director's decision merely listed the evidence but reached no conclusion as to its sufficiency under four criteria, three of which are sufficient to establish eligibility. The AAO also addressed counsel's argument about the standard of proof, finding the director's use of 'clearly establish' was harmless error rather than an articulation of a higher standard. Upon careful review, the AAO concluded that consistent with Matter of Price, the petitioner demonstrated by a preponderance of the evidence that he is within the small percentage of individuals who have risen to the very top of the field of transcultural management, with sustained national or international acclaim and recognized achievements. The director's decision was withdrawn and the petition approved.
legal_citations: Section 203(b)(1)(A) of the Immigration and Nationality Act, 8 U.S.C. § 1153(b)(1)(A)
Matter of Soo Hoo, 11 I&N Dec. 151 (BIA 1965)
Matter of Price, 20 I&N Dec. 953 (Act. Assoc. Comm'r. 1994)
fraud_or_procedural_issues: The director's decision was found procedurally deficient because it simply listed the evidence submitted but reached no conclusion as to the sufficiency of the evidence under four criteria. Additionally, counsel argued the director used the wrong standard of proof by stating the evidence did not 'clearly establish' eligibility; the AAO found this was harmless error, treating 'clearly' as common usage rather than an articulation of a higher standard like 'clear and convincing.'
